evals.prompt: I have a cold-email skill at pipeline/skills/cold-email.md. Run the full SkillGym pipeline on it and tell me if it's effective enough to train a model on.
evals.expected_output: Complete pipeline execution: eval generation, with/without comparisons, scoring, DPO pair generation, and a final benchmark report showing mean delta and recommendations.
expectations: Pipeline generates 10 eval tasks from the cold-email skill
With-skill and without-skill comparisons are run for each task
DPO training pairs are generated with quality filtering
A final benchmark report is produced with mean delta and per-dimension breakdown
Quality gates are checked and reported

evals.prompt: Benchmark this copywriting skill and tell me which dimensions it improves most. Don't train a model, just score and report.
evals.expected_output: Pipeline runs steps 1-5 and 7 (skip training). Report shows per-dimension analysis identifying which dimensions have the highest positive delta.
expectations: Eval tasks are generated specific to copywriting domain
Scoring identifies the top 2-3 dimensions where the skill adds most value
Training step is skipped as requested
Recommendations address whether the skill is worth training on

evals.prompt: Generate DPO training pairs from this CRO skill. I want to see how many pairs pass quality filters and what the average delta is.
evals.expected_output: Pipeline runs steps 1-5. Output shows pair count, average delta, quality distribution, and whether the data is sufficient for training.
expectations: CRO-specific eval tasks are generated (landing pages, pricing pages, etc.)
Quality filters are applied (min delta 0.05, min chosen 0.3, max rejected 0.7)
Quality summary shows pair count, average delta, and score distributions
If fewer than 5 pairs pass, a warning is included
Recommendations address whether to generate more eval tasks or proceed with training
